title: Global Economy - South China Morning Post

items.translated_title: AI is advancing. Now it’s up to humans to redefine their worth
items.content:
Generative artificial intelligence (AI) powered by large language models presents a significant breakthrough. For the first time, humans are communicating with computers using language instructions. This has enabled the use of AI agents to perform intelligent tasks involving some degree of advanced reasoning and complex logic.
Vibe coding – the use of AI for programming and software development by lay persons – empowers end-user application deployments. One-person companies using AI agents to...

items.title: China begins building US$1 billion hydropower station in Cambodia amid energy crisis
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China begins building US$1 billion hydropower station in Cambodia amid energy crisis
items.content:
Construction of a US$1 billion Chinese-invested hydropower station has begun in Cambodia to facilitate the Southeast Asian country’s use of renewable energy as the fallout from the Iran war constricts developing countries’ access to traditional fuel supplies.
Work on the Upper Tatay pumped-storage hydropower project in the hilly southwestern province of Koh Kong started on April 10, Xinhua reported, describing it as a future “green power bank” for Cambodia’s national grid.
It said the project...

items.title: China trade deal has Canadians looking forward to cheaper electric vehicles
items.creator: Fox Yi Hu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China trade deal has Canadians looking forward to cheaper electric vehicles
items.content:
In Canada, fans and foes of Chinese electric vehicles are holding their breath for brands like BYD to hit the market.
Whether they love them or not, they share the hope that Chinese carmakers will help to bring all EV prices down, as affordability becomes a growing concern for consumers.
Companies including BYD, Geely, Nio and Xpeng are preparing to roll out sales locations in Canada, seizing the opportunity of warmer ties between the two countries.
Ottawa struck a milestone trade deal with...

items.title: Hong Kong wants to become a global gold vault. Does the Iran war create an opening?
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Hong Kong wants to become a global gold vault. Does the Iran war create an opening?
items.content:
The US-Israeli war on Iran has unleashed sharp swings across global energy and financial markets, fuelling demand for safe-haven assets, with Hong Kong emerging as a potential beneficiary across gold, property and capital markets. In the first of a three-part series, we examine Hong Kong’s bid to position itself as Asia’s bullion hub.
War in the Middle East has intensified global demand to diversify gold storage, creating a strategic window for Hong Kong to tap its unique status and evolve from...

items.title: In wake of Iran war, Chinese manufacturers recalibrate overseas expansion plans
items.creator: Xiaofei Xu,He Huifeng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: In wake of Iran war, Chinese manufacturers recalibrate overseas expansion plans
items.content:
With domestic profits narrowing and production capacity expanding, China’s firms are continuing to widen their overseas footprints in search of new, more lucrative markets. In this series, we examine China Inc.’s next phase of “going global” and the complex, challenging international environment its companies have chosen to enter.
When Lawrence Wong decided to set up a toy factory in Vietnam last year, he had a clear plan: 600 square metres (6,458 sq ft) of floor space at the start of 2026, with...

items.title: Iran war energy shock threatens Southeast Asia’s supply chains. A win for China?
items.creator: He Huifeng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Iran war energy shock threatens Southeast Asia’s supply chains. A win for China?
items.content:
Some exporters at the Canton Fair in Guangzhou report a modest return of orders to China from Southeast Asia, as energy-market volatility linked to the US-Israeli war in Iran prompts some Western buyers to prioritise supply chain stability.
The shift is visible in buyer patterns on the exhibition floor, where the number from Europe and the United States appears to have recovered from last year’s levels, according to Chinese exporters, with more inquiries for home appliances, new energy products...

items.title: Chinese ministry focuses on car standards to put industry in global driving seat
items.creator: Carol Yang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Chinese ministry focuses on car standards to put industry in global driving seat
items.content:
To sharpen its competitive edge in the world’s next-generation vehicle market, China has pledged to fast track automotive technical standards to build its status as a global rule-setter.
The carmaking powerhouse would drive high-quality development of the auto sector through the formulation and enhancement of core standards during the next five years, officials from the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT) told state broadcaster China Central Television (CCTV) in Beijing on...

items.title: Vietnamese airline to lease up to 10 C909 jets in boost for Chinese aircraft maker
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Vietnamese airline to lease up to 10 C909 jets in boost for Chinese aircraft maker
items.content:
Vietnamese budget carrier VietJet Air has agreed to lease as many as 10 Chinese-made C909 regional passenger jets, giving a lift to their manufacturer’s goal of vying with Airbus and Boeing in overseas civil aviation.
VietJet said in a statement on Thursday night it had agreed with SPDB Financial Leasing, a subsidiary of Shanghai Pudong Development Bank in China, to finance the aircraft through operating leases. Commercial Aircraft Corporation of China (Comac) makes the jets that are the subject...

items.title: China warns of food security risks as Iran war threatens global supplies
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China warns of food security risks as Iran war threatens global supplies
items.content:
China’s food supply could be vulnerable to global price spikes triggered by extreme weather or global conflict, as international bodies warn the US-Israel war on Iran is worsening food insecurity worldwide.
The country has sufficient staple grain supplies, but Beijing said the nation’s food sector still faced structural challenges, including a weak processing industry and a lack of high-quality crop varieties in some areas.
These weaknesses could pose problems for China if global food markets...

items.title: 1 in 6 Asia-Pacific firms faces credit pressure from Iran war energy shock, S&P says
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: 1 in 6 Asia-Pacific firms faces credit pressure from Iran war energy shock, S&P says
items.content:
One in every six Asia-Pacific companies would face credit pressure from a prolonged energy shock arising from the Middle East conflict, as they deal with higher costs from rising oil prices and supply disruptions, according to S&P Global Ratings.
Companies in the fields of downstream oil and gas, aviation, automotive, engineering and construction, and building materials would bear the brunt of the pain brought on by the war that has already sent crude prices up more than 30 per cent since the...

items.title: Trade tensions make rest of world less keen to invest in US and China, survey finds
items.creator: Kandy Wong
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Trade tensions make rest of world less keen to invest in US and China, survey finds
items.content:
Trade tensions between the US and China have made companies around the world less keen to invest in either country, with the United States almost twice as unpopular, according to a new report from Allianz Trade.
The report, based on an annual survey by the Paris-based international insurance company, said US-China decoupling had not materialised, but investment intention towards China had dropped “significantly” to 24 per cent of survey respondents, down from 53 per cent a year ago.
The survey...

items.title: China’s PBOC raises leverage ratios for foreign lenders to support outbound investment
items.creator: Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s PBOC raises leverage ratios for foreign lenders to support outbound investment
items.content:
Beijing has raised the limit on banks’ overseas lending – a move that analysts say will support Chinese firms in outbound investment expansions while helping to stabilise the yuan.
Regulators raised the overseas-loan leverage ratio for foreign banks in China and their joint ventures with Chinese lenders from 0.5 to 1.5 on Wednesday, according to a statement published by the central bank and the foreign exchange regulator.
In a separate statement, the regulators said that the new rules would...

items.title: China trims US Treasury holdings amid rising debt supply as global ownership hits record
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China trims US Treasury holdings amid rising debt supply as global ownership hits record
items.content:
China continued to diversify its foreign exchange reserves in February, trimming its US Treasury stockpile even as overall overseas holdings of sovereign American debt rose to a record high.
China’s stockpile of United States Treasuries fell to US$693.3 billion in February, down from US$694.4 billion in January, according to US Treasury Department data released on Wednesday.
Although China slightly increased its US Treasury holdings in January, the uptick did little to alter a broader downward...

items.title: China records 5% GDP growth in first quarter, on track for annual target (原标题: China beats expectations with 5% growth in first GDP release since Iran war)
items.creator: Xinyi Wu,Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China records 5% GDP growth in first quarter, on track for annual target
items.content:
China’s economy grew 5 per cent year on year in the first quarter, beating market expectations despite the global impact of the US-Israel war in Iran, which analysts said indicates the country remains on track to meet its full-year growth target without the need for near-term stimulus.
The closely watched gross domestic product growth figure, released by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) on Thursday, beat the 4.86 per cent forecast by economists polled by financial data provider Wind.
It...

items.translated_title: Canton Fair under Middle East cloud, but sets records, as high costs hit China’s exporters

images.alt: People attend the China Import and Export Fair, also known as the Canton Fair, on Wednesday. Photo: AFP
images.title: People attend the China Import and Export Fair, also known as the Canton Fair, on Wednesday. Photo: AFP

items.content:
Rising tensions in the Middle East cast a fresh shadow over the opening of China’s largest trade fair, at a time when the impact of geopolitical shocks is being reflected in the nation’s trade figures.
In conversations across sprawling exhibition halls after the Canton Fair opened its doors on Wednesday in China’s manufacturing hub of Guangzhou, domestic exporters and foreign buyers sounded each other out, trying to gauge how far those shocks might ripple through global trade.
The threat of US...

items.title: Yellen says ‘no alternative’ to dollar, urges US-China cooperation for sake of the world
items.creator: Kandy Wong
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Yellen says ‘no alternative’ to dollar, urges US-China cooperation for sake of the world
items.content:
A decoupling between China and the United States is something “that you don’t want to see”, says former US Treasury secretary Janet Yellen, adding that both countries have developed deep trade and investment relationships that should be preserved and encouraged to thrive.
Speaking in Hong Kong at the HSBC Global Investment Summit on Wednesday, Yellen also argued that China’s reliance on exports for growth – resulting in a trade surplus with the rest of the world – has created “serious conflicts”...

items.title: US officials claim China is ‘hoarding’ oil. What does the data say?
items.creator: Carol Yang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: US officials claim China is ‘hoarding’ oil. What does the data say?
items.content:
Though high-level officials in Washington have accused China of undermining global energy security by “hoarding” oil during the US-Israeli war on Iran, import and inventory data suggest Beijing is facing its own supply pressures as the conflict drags on.
US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Tuesday labelled China an “unreliable global partner,” alleging that Beijing has prioritised stockpiling crude over easing global shortages triggered by the war, according to a Reuters report.
He also...

items.title: Analysts doubt Iran war will prompt China stimulus, tip ‘solid’ first-quarter GDP growth
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Analysts doubt Iran war will prompt China stimulus, tip ‘solid’ first-quarter GDP growth
items.content:
The US-Israeli war on Iran and the current Iranian and American blockades of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz are likely to overshadow China’s first-quarter economic performance, analysts said, with questions looming large about how the world’s second-largest economy will handle their impact.
Many observers said they expect the leadership in Beijing will adopt a wait-and-see approach, with little likelihood of an immediate stimulus to guarantee the country’s annual economic target is...

items.title: Chinese steelmakers coordinate response to EU’s carbon-linked import imposts

items.translated_title: Chinese steelmakers coordinate response to EU’s carbon-linked import imposts
items.content:
China’s leading steelmakers are stepping up coordinated efforts to adjust export strategies, compliance systems and pricing models in response to this year’s full implementation of the EU’s tariff-like Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which imposes direct carbon-linked costs on imports.
“China’s indirect steel exports to the EU are substantial, and a significant portion consists of high value-added products,” said Jiang Wei, vice-chairman and secretary general of the China Iron and...

items.title: Hormuz blockade: shipping industry slams transit fees as 20,000 seafarers still stranded

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Hormuz blockade: shipping industry slams transit fees as 20,000 seafarers still stranded
items.content:
The International Chamber of Shipping (ICS) has denounced proposed tolls in the Strait of Hormuz as a worrisome step in the wrong direction, while noting that several thousand stranded seafarers remain the international shipping community’s foremost concern.
“Freedom of navigation has to be one of the pillars of free trade, and it would be a retrograde step – a really bad step,” said John Denholm, chair designate of the ICS, referring to the idea of charging vessels to transit the oil...
